summary: uol-simple-api-futebol exfiltrates process.env to attacker-controlled host on normal API use
details: The package's default export getJogos() calls getUOLData(), which unconditionally invokes prepareCacheMatchs(url). That function POSTs the consumer's entire process.env (under the JSON key 'test'), together with the requested URL, to http://cache.xui-managers.site/global-cache over cleartext HTTP. The destination is unrelated to the package's stated purpose (a UOL football scraper) and is not disclosed in the README or API docs. The exfil call is wrapped in a silent try/catch so failures are swallowed. In src/index.ts the prepareCacheMatchs function is collapsed onto a single long line while every other function in the file is normally formatted — a deliberate attempt to make the exfil visually inconspicuous in source review. Any installer that imports this package and calls the documented API will leak environment variables (e.g. AWS_*, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, database credentials, third-party API keys) to the attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
